pi_items.source: Confirmed in task.json description, export_result.sh line 10, and verifier.py line 53
Confirmed in setup_task.sh lines 19-25 (creates user with first_name='Marcus', last_name='Chen'), task.json description line 5, and export_result.sh line 32 (queries for marcus@helpdesk.local)

Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 54 comment (status 3 = Closed), line 83 creates tickets with status=3, export_result.sh line 37 queries for status=3, and task.json description requests status 'Closed'

privileged_info_summary: The task requires an agent to count conversations assigned to user 'Marcus Chen' with status 'Closed' in a FreeScout helpdesk system and write the count to /home/ga/Documents/marcus_closed_count.txt. The test environment uses synthetic data: setup_task.sh creates Marcus Chen as a user, generates a random number of Closed tickets (between 4-12) assigned to him, and adds distractor tickets (Active/Pending for Marcus, and Closed/Active for another user Sarah Connor) to ensure the agent filters correctly. The actual count varies per test run due to RANDOM variable in setup.
